message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Birba (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,300+ reviews; Tripadvisor 500+ reviews; OpenTable reservation volume present (exact count unverified). Composite: 1,500-2,000+ across major platforms.
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Opened January 2011; 15 years operating under continuous ownership by Tara Lazar / F10 Hospitality with husband Marco Rossetti.
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice (top 10% ranking, currently #44 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants)","Listed in Time Out's best restaurants in Palm Springs roundup","Listed in Bon Traveler's 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs","Listed in Visit Palm Springs Uptown Design District guide","Featured in LA Magazine profile of owner Tara Lazar","Featured in Locale Magazine 'Italian Restaurants in the Coachella Valley' roundup","goop place listing"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best Italian restaurants Palm Springs', 'wood-fired pizza Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs Italian patio', 'Uptown Design District restaurants'. Does NOT consistently top regional queries like 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' (wrong city), and is overshadowed by Johnny Costa's, Sammy G's, and Il Corso in many AI-generated Italian shortlists despite being a critical-favorite. Often listed in second tier of LLM Italian roundups even when it deserves anchor placement.
- Site structured data: Primary website birbaf10.com is behind a Cloudflare bot-challenge (HTTP 403 'cf-mitigated: challenge' to non-browser user agents). The site is effectively invisible to AI agents, schema validators, and most crawlers. No JSON-LD or microdata could be confirmed because the page body is not served to agents. Hours, menu, address, and structured signals cannot be read by an LLM directly — agents must rely on third-party aggregators (Yelp, OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Visit Palm Springs) for facts.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Birba in Palm Springs and its connection to Tara Lazar:

## Restaurant Opening and History

Birba opened in 2011 as an al fresco Italian restaurant crafting handmade pastas and pizzas made with respect to Tara Lazar's Italian cooking training. The restaurant is owned by Marco Rossetti and his wife/Executive Chef Tara Lazar, who also own the adjacent Cheeky's.

## Restaurant Features

Since 2011, Birba has been serving up handcrafted seasonal cocktails, homemade pizza and pasta in an almost entirely outdoor setting. The gorgeous star lit patio serves as the quintessential Palm Springs experience and regularly transforms into a buzzing scene showcasing live music by local artists.

## Tara Lazar's Background

After playing tennis at UC Berkeley, working in finance in San Francisco, and living abroad in Costa Rica, Tara along with her husband Marco embarked on coming home to Palm Springs. Cheeky's opened its doors in 2008 focusing on breakfast classics with food from local farms and a whimsical menu that still changes weekly, before Birba followed three years later.

Based on the search results, here's information about Birba, Palm Springs, F10 Hospitality, and the owners:

F10 Hospitality is owned by local chef, restaurateur and hotelier Tara Lazar. Tara, along with her husband Marco, embarked on coming home to Palm Springs, where they founded the hospitality group.

They formed F10 – an eclectic, creative and "cheeky" tribe of hospitality kinfolk, with Alcazar Palm Springs being part of F10 alongside Cheeky's, Birba, Mr. Lyons, Seymours, and F10 Catering + Events.

The first Birba opened on Palm Canyon Drive in downtown Palm Springs in 2011 with a second outpost in Cabazon's Desert Hills Premium Outlets following in 2024. Birba provides evening dining ambience with exquisite cocktails and a modern Italian menu.

F10 Hospitality has grown to include Cheeky's, Birba PSP, Birba CBZ, Alcazar (a 34-room boutique hotel), F10 Catering + Events, Mr. Lyons, Seymour's, High/Low, and Tack Room Tavern in Indio, CA.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Birba in the Time Out Palm Springs best restaurants guide:

## Birba - Featured in Time Out's Best Restaurants

Birba is a family-friendly restaurant best known for its spicy pork meatballs, and it's included in Time Out's list of the best restaurants in Palm Springs.

### What's Recommended

The Birba pizza is a solid choice with toppings like prosciutto, honey, chili oil, and mozzarella. The non-alcoholic cocktails are definitely worth checking out if you're on the wagon.

### Dining Experience

Birba offers alfresco dining featuring Italian specialties with a central fire pit that creates a charming ambiance while serving pasta, pizzas, and creative cocktails. It offers many dietary options like cauliflower crust, zucchini noodles, and others.

### Special Tip from Time Out

Time Out recommends asking about their current Piece of the Pie, a rotating specialty pizza that's sold to raise funds for select local nonprofits.

### Additional Location

Birba has a newest location at the Desert Hills Premium Outlets in Cabazon.

I found a detailed LA Magazine profile of Tara Lazar featuring Birba. Here's what I found:

First with Cheeky's, then Birba, and now a reinvented steak house called Mr. Lyons, Tara Lazar has updated the area's cuisine for the first time since the Rat Pack era.

The profile covers Lazar's entrepreneurial journey in Palm Springs. She was a stock trader during her 20s, moved back to Palm Springs and was annoyed there wasn't any good breakfast. The lines stretching to the curb gave Lazar the confidence three years later to open a sister restaurant, Birba, a stylish cantina with a wood-burning pizza oven and excellent cocktails. With long, cushiony benches around outdoor fire pits and a good-looking young crowd, the place became the boozy civic hub of the burgeoning Uptown Design District.

The article also notes that Tara's very relaxed, but if you go for early breakfast, she's the one making your omelette, and you'll probably see her again at Birba at the end of the night, according to her friend, clothing designer Trina Turk.

Perfect! I found the information you were looking for. Birba is a Palm Springs institution offering chic Italian food when you need a slice of pizza or a hearty bowl of pasta. The restaurant is featured in Bon Traveler's article on the 20 best restaurants in Palm Springs.

According to the Bon Traveler article, Birba has a wonderful outdoor patio with spacious seating available, and their craft cocktails are wonderfully paired with their ever-changing menu.

Additional details about Birba:
- Since 2011, Birba has been serving up handcrafted seasonal cocktails, homemade pizza and pasta in an almost entirely outdoor setting.
- They specialize in homemade Milanese and Tuscan-style food and source all their ingredients from small and local farms.
- The atmosphere is casual, chic and lively, an al-fresco garden setting with string lights, heaters and fire pits.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Birba and its coverage in Locale Magazine:

Birba is an Italian restaurant located in Palm Springs that specializes in homemade Milanese and Tuscan-style food. The restaurant has been in operation since 2011.

Birba serves craft cocktails, homemade pizza and pasta, with ingredients sourced from farms right in Coachella Valley. Birba is described as the ideal place to dine for a laid-back, alfresco dinner, and has been open for over a decade.

The search results show that Birba was featured in Locale Magazine's article about Italian restaurants in the Coachella Valley. The article highlighted the restaurant's commitment to sourcing fresh, local ingredients and serving authentic Italian cuisine in the desert region.

Links: [{"title":"Uptown Design District Palm Springs | Shopping, Restaurants & Guide","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/uptown-design-district-guide/"},{"title":"Welcome - Birba | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.birbaf10.com/"},{"title":"The Alcazar Experience - Alcazar Hotel","url":"https://alcazarpalmsprings.com/the-alcazar-experience/"},{"title":"uptown Archives - Visit Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/tag/uptown/feed/"},{"title":"7 Uptown Design District Restaurants to try in Palm Springs - Orange Coast","url":"https://orangecoast.com/2019/7-uptown-design-district-restaurants-to-try-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Best Restaurants in Palm Springs' Uptown Design District","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/best-restaurants-in-palm-springs-uptown-design-district/"},{"title":"Birba | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/birba/24650/"},{"title":"Birba Brunch - Birba Palm Springs","url":"https://birbaps.com/birba-brunch/"},{"title":"Birba Restaurant Palm Springs CA Reviews | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/birba-palm-springs-ca-92262_35ps110507.html"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Birba in Palm Springs' Uptown Design District:

## Birba Overview

Birba is an Italian restaurant serving handmade pasta and wood-fired pizza, located in the Uptown Design District. The name "Birba" roughly translates to "scoundrel" in Italian.

## Menu and Dining Experience

The restaurant serves up handcrafted cocktails, handmade pasta, wood-fired pizza, elegant salads, and their famous gluten-free cauliflower pizza in an inviting tree-filled patio. While pizza is its calling card, guests may also choose from pasta, salads and meaty dishes, like Nonna's Meatballs and crispy pork belly, with pizzas offered in flavor combinations that take this traditional dish to an entirely different level.

## Atmosphere and Amenities

Birba is an Uptown Design District restaurant with a high octane vibe, sexy crowd and creative cocktails. The restaurant also offers weekend brunch service featuring breakfast pizzas, colorful salads, and hand-crafted morning cocktails with a full bar available.

## Location

It's nestled next to the breakfast and lunch venue Cheeky's and is owned by the same couple, and shares a street address.

**Current Ranking:** Birba is "#44 of 281 Restaurants in Palm Springs"

**Total Reviews:** The restaurant has accumulated 517 reviews on the platform.

**Travelers' Choice Award:** Yes, Birba carries a Travelers' Choice award. According to Tripadvisor's criteria, this recognition goes to establishments that "consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor."

**Overall Rating:** The restaurant maintains a 4.1 out of 5 bubbles rating.

Birba is known for excellent woodfired pizza with a side of SCENE. "Birba" roughly translates to "scoundrel" in Italian, and it's nestled next to the breakfast and lunch venue Cheeky's and is owned by the same couple.

The restaurant is located in the Uptown Design District and features a high octane vibe, sexy crowd and creative cocktails. Birba's atmosphere is casual, chic and lively, with an al‑fresco garden setting with string lights, heaters and fire pits, and features a buzzy, social crowd.

Birba is a cocktail bar and, while pizza is definitely its calling card, guests may also choose from pasta, salads and meaty dishes, like Nonna's Meatballs and crispy pork belly.

content.text: I have sufficient information. The Yelp review count from the title snippet shows 1,347 reviews (matches the 1,300+ claim). Let me finalize.

confirms: Opened in 2011 under Tara Lazar and her husband Marco Rossetti as part of what is now F10 Hospitality (continuous ownership, ~15 years operating); multiple primary sources including LA Magazine, F10's own site, and Alcazar Palm Springs confirm this.
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award AND #44 of 281 Palm Springs ranking verified directly on Tripadvisor restaurant page.
Yelp review volume of 1,300+ verified (1,347 reviews shown in Yelp page title).
Tripadvisor review volume of 500+ verified (517 reviews on Tripadvisor page).
Listed in Time Out's 14 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs roundup (timeout.com).
Listed in Bon Traveler's '20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs' (bontraveler.com).
Featured in LA Magazine profile of Tara Lazar covering Cheeky's, Birba, and Mr. Lyons (lamag.com/digestblog/565443).
Featured in Locale Magazine's '9 Italian Restaurants in the Coachella Valley' roundup (localemagazine.com).
goop place listing confirmed at goop.com/place/palm-springs-guide/birba.
Listed in Visit Palm Springs Uptown Design District guide (visitpalmsprings.com).
contradicts: The brief states Locale Magazine roundup was titled 'Italian Restaurants in the Coachella Valley' — actual title is '9 Italian Restaurants in the Coachella Valley for Your Fix of Pizza and Pasta'; minor — substance of the claim still holds.
Brief lists Bon Traveler roundup as '20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs' — verified as exact match, no contradiction. (Removing — confirmed.)

Composite '1,500-2,000+ across major platforms' — Yelp 1,347 + Tripadvisor 517 already exceeds 1,800; plausible but depends on OpenTable & Google review counts not independently retrieved here.
Sentiment 'mostly positive' — Tripadvisor rating is 4.1/5 (consistent with mostly positive) but Yelp aggregate not independently retrieved.
Visibility-gap score of 7 and convertibility tier 'convertible' — these are internal scoring constructs, not externally verifiable.
Claim that birbaf10.com returns a Cloudflare bot-challenge to non-browser agents — not independently re-tested in this audit lens.

Specific 2026 ranking '#44 of 281' — Tripadvisor currently shows #44 of 281 (verified); however earlier search snippets showed #49 of 308, suggesting ranking fluctuates. Snapshot accurate as of fetch but not stable.

<parameter name="notes">No James Beard nomination or Michelin recognition was claimed by the brief, and independent search confirms Birba has neither — the brief is appropriately modest. All press/credential claims (Time Out, Bon Traveler, LA Magazine, Locale Magazine, goop, Visit Palm Springs, Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice) verify against primary sources. Opening year, ownership (Tara Lazar / Marco Rossetti), F10 Hospitality affiliation, and 15-year continuous operation all hold up. The only real soft spots are the OpenTable volume and composite review-total estimate, both of which are reasonable but not independently nailed down. Note: Tara Lazar is Executive Chef and primary public-facing owner; Marco Rossetti is co-owner — some sources frame Tara as the lead operator, which is consistent with brief's wording.
